description_long: This is a modern military jet aircraft with a sleek, aerodynamic fuselage and a delta wing configuration. The body is predominantly gray with roundel insignias on the wings and tail, indicating military use. The cockpit is positioned forward and features a clear, multi-panel canopy, likely accommodating one or two crew members. The aircraft is equipped with twin jet engines mounted at the rear, and the landing gear is visible in a tricycle arrangement with two main wheels and a nose wheel. The wings are sharply swept back and blend smoothly into the fuselage, with additional hardpoints for external stores or armaments. The tail section features a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers, forming a conventional tail design.
description: A gray, delta-wing military jet with a forward cockpit, twin rear jet engines, and a tricycle landing gear. Features include swept wings, a single vertical tail, and visible military insignia.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the delta wing shape, forward cockpit, and roundel insignias on the wings and tail.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the sleek fuselage, forward canopy, and the tricycle landing gear with visible underwing stores.
description_view_2: Rear-bottom view displaying the twin jet exhausts, main landing gear, and the integration of the wings with the fuselage.
description_view_3: Top-side view emphasizing the overall planform, single vertical stabilizer, and the placement of the cockpit and wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, delta configuration with sharply swept wings and no visible winglets; wings blend smoothly into the fuselage.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines, rear-mounted with visible exhausts.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle, retractable landing gear with two main gear units and one nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a multi-panel canopy, likely single or tandem seat.
